name: Stuffed Squid with Vinegar/Soy Dip - Ojingu C
recipeInstructions:
1. Clean the squid under running water and remove the clear inner quill.
Pull away the head and tentacles, then cut off the tentacles just above the head.
Remove the skin and discard the little flaps. Wash the squid thoroughly.
2. Finely chop the tentacles and sauté in vegetable oil with the meat,
cabbage, bean sprouts, green onion, and garlic until cooked through.
3. Drain the mushrooms, squeezing out as much water as possible, then remove the stems
and finely chop the caps. Add to the pan and stir fry for 1 minute.
4. Add soy sauce, sesame oil, salt, and pepper; cook until well combined.
5. Fill the squid with the prepared stuffing, securing the opening with wooden picks.
6. Place in a lightly oiled dish, sprinkle with extra sesame oil, and steam over rapidly boiling water
for about 20 minutes. Remove from the steamer and cut into thick slices.
7. Serve with Vinegar/Soy Dip.
Vinegar/Soy Dip:
3/4 cup Light Soy Sauce
1/4 cup White Vinegar
1/4 cup Toasted White Sesame Seeds
2 tsp Finely Chopped Green Onions
